mn-mulapariyayavagga.englishSubtitle: The Chapter on the Root of All Things
mn-mulapariyayavagga.description: This chapter, though beginning with the abstruse Mūlapariyāya Sutta, mostly contains foundational teachings and can, as a whole, serve as an introduction to the discourses.

mn-sihanadavagga.englishSubtitle: The Chapter on the Lion's Roar
mn-sihanadavagga.description: Beginning with two discourses containing a "lion's roar", this chapter deals with suffering, community life, and practical meditation advice.

mn-opammavagga.description: A diverse chapter including biography, non-violence, not-self, and an influential teaching on the progress of meditation.

mn-mahayamakavagga.description: Discourses arranged as pairs of longer and shorter texts.

mn-culayamakavagga.description: A similar arrangement to the previous chapter, with paired longer and shorter discourses on key doctrines.

mn-gahapativagga.description: This chapter is addressed to a diverse range of lay people.

mn-bhikkhuvagga.description: Ten discourses to monks, many of them focusing on matters of discipline.

mn-paribbajakavagga.description: The Buddha in dialog with ascetics and wanderers.

mn-rajavagga.description: Various dialogs with kings and princes, many of whom followed the Buddha.

mn-brahmanavagga.description: The Buddha engages with the powerful caste of brahmins, contesting their claims to spiritual authority.

mn-devadahavagga.description: Diverse teachings opening with the Devadaha Sutta.

mn-anupadavagga.englishSubtitle: The Chapter Beginning with One By One
mn-anupadavagga.description: Many of the discourses in this chapter delve into complex and analytical presentations of core teachings. It includes important discourses on meditation.

mn-sunnatavagga.description: Named after the first discourses, which deal with emptiness, this chapter presents less analytical and more narrative texts.

mn-vibhangavagga.description: A series of discourses presented as technical analyses of shorter teachings.

mn-salayatanavagga.englishSubtitle: The Chapter on the Six Senses
mn-salayatanavagga.description: Most discourses in this chapter deal with the six sense fields.
